title: RestoreGuard audit report
description: The stable machine-readable report emitted by `restoreguard --json`. Field names are a contract: additive changes only within a schemaVersion; a breaking change bumps schemaVersion and ships a new schema file. `additionalProperties` is permissive by design so a consumer validating an OLDER schema against a NEWER (additively-grown) report still passes.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this report contract. Constant 1 for documents matching this schema.

generatedAt.description: When the audit was generated (DateTimeOffset, ISO 8601 with offset).

overall.description: The audit's overall verdict — the max severity across findings.

partial.description: True when discovery was incomplete (one or more providerErrors); a partial run must NOT be read as a clean bill of health.

target.description: The connection id (from reporting.json) this COPY of the report was written to; null for the stdout copy. Distinct per destination, so a stored report is self-describing about where it lives. Additive since schemaVersion 1.

hash.description: SHA-256 (hex) of the report core — every field EXCEPT target and hash. Identical across all copies of one audit, so a reader can dedup a report it finds in multiple targets. Additive since schemaVersion 1.

findings.description: Active findings, one per failed/degraded check.

suppressedFindings.description: Findings matched by an active suppression — rendered in their own section, never silently dropped.

providerErrors.description: Human-readable discovery errors (e.g. 'lab118: unreachable'). Non-empty implies partial=true.
